svg.aria-label: MemFleet — what happens when agents share a coordination layer
svg.text: MEASURED · 475 AGENTS · 30 OVERLAP ZONES · K=2 / K=5 / K=10
When a coding fleet shares a coordination layer,

g.text: Almost every collision that would have
paged a dev now resolves itself.
Same outcome, in half the time —
your fleet ships before lunch, not after.
One agent's work covers the whole fleet.
The other nine save tokens and avoid stomping.
OF BASELINE TOKENS BURN ON WORK
~93M of 103M tokens · ~$18 of every $20 spent · 271 of 300 agents collide
redundant agents skip before they ever spawn
Bench measured: −25.7% tokens (subprocess cold-start floors it) · Production projection: −90%
HOW IT SCALES — MORE AGENTS, BIGGER PAYOFF
The more agents you run in parallel, the more
Coordination overhead: 0.01% of runtime · 1,040 nodes protected at K=10
